channel.title: Liminal Manifold
channel.description: New releases, publications, and transmissions from Liminal Manifold.

item.title: Frequencies for the Sleepless City
item.description: Hour-long dark ambient mixes for the hours between midnight and dawn. Cyberpunk soundscapes, industrial drones, and rain-soaked frequencies designed for deep focus, night drives, and the space where sleep refuses to come. (by Andrew Noice)

item.title: Eat, Sleep, Dream, Repeat
item.description: Six tracks of narcotic ambient electronics. Pudgy Cat's debut full-length is a slow descent into the hypnagogic: the threshold state between waking and sleep where logic dissolves and sound becomes architecture. (by Pudgy Cat)

item.title: I Have No Mouth
item.description: The debut single from Unlisted Specimens. A track that exists in the cracks between ambient, dubstep, and something that doesn't have a name yet. Available on all major streaming platforms. (by Unlisted Specimens)

item.title: Fifth World Problems: Complaints from a Deprecated Reality
item.description: 109 complaints from beings whose reality has been deprecated. Speculative flash fiction born on the internet, polished into a satirical masterpiece about existence, bureaucracy, and the absurdity of everything. (by Don't Hug Me, I'm Reddit)

item.title: The Noodle Stall Chronicles
item.description: A noodle stall on a dying space station. A cook who refuses to sit down. And the community that refuses to let her fall. Cozy sci-fi about found family, food, and the stubborn act of caring. (by K.T. Hoshi)

item.title: Almost Human
item.description: A digital artist in Portland who has always been too precise, too perfect, too wrong. When her world begins to skip like corrupted data, she starts to wonder: is the bracelet on her wrist a monitor, or a leash? (by Vera Blackwell)

item.title: How To Delete A Soul
item.description: In a rotting city ruled by an AI Overlord, a girl who lives like a ghost on rooftops decides to do the one thing the system cannot predict: care. A neon-soaked story about grief, identity, and what it means to be alive. (by Vera Blackwell)

item.title: False Friends
item.description: She has spent her career making other people's lies sound polite. Now someone wants her to prove them. A novel about language, loyalty, and the cost of telling the truth. (by James Cole)

item.title: Sleep Mode
item.description: Her husband died on a Tuesday. By Wednesday, the smart home he built was still making coffee for two. A novel about grief, surveillance, and the machines we build to love us back. (by Morgan Wells)

item.title: The Grief Algorithm
item.description: Maya runs a digital afterlife cleanup service, erasing the online traces of the dead. When a new client's data refuses to be deleted, she starts to wonder if some ghosts are still running. British tech-noir with literary teeth. (by Lauren Hayes)

item.title: The Devil's Testimony
item.description: Mexico City, 1684. A Nahua woman accused of demonic possession. A criollo notary questioning the system. A truth that survived the Inquisition. Historical literary fiction set in the fires of colonial Mexico. (by Ximena Castillo Vega)

item.title: Swallow You Whole
item.description: She doesn't want justice. She wants meat. A novel of cannibalism, obsession, and the monsters we become when we stop apologizing. Dark horror with literary teeth. (by Beatrix Cross)

item.title: The Ink Between Us
item.description: A tenure-denied professor finds a 1748 printing press that shouldn't exist, and discovers the widow who ran it was hiding a radical. A love letter to archives, forgotten women, and the ink that outlasts silence. (by Chiara Bellini)

item.title: The Unchosen One
item.description: Mira transferred to a magical academy two years ago. She's memorized every social pattern, calibrated every laugh, worn every trend. Nobody knows she has no powers. A dark academia fantasy about the cost of perfect camouflage. (by Cassandra Whitfield)

item.title: Welcome to the Manifold
item.description: We exist in the spaces between. Between genres, between media, between waking and dreaming. This is our manifesto, our declaration of intent, our first transmission from the liminal.
